Given a list of N integers and a rotation value K, rotate the list left by K positions.

Left rotation means moving the first K elements to the end.
problems.inputFormat:
First line: Two integers N and K.
Second line: N space-separated integers.
problems.outputFormat: Print the rotated list as space-separated integers.
problems.explanation: Left rotate by 2: Move first 2 elements (1,2) to end. Result: [3,4,5,1,2].

problems.title: Fibonacci Series Generator
problems.description:
Given a positive integer N, generate and print the first N terms of the Fibonacci series.

Fibonacci series: 0, 1, 1, 2, 3, 5, 8, 13, ...
problems.inputFormat: A single positive integer N.
problems.outputFormat: Print the first N Fibonacci numbers, space-separated.

problems.title: Check Palindrome Number
problems.description:
Given an integer N, check if it is a palindrome number.

A palindrome number reads the same forwards and backwards. Negative numbers are not palindromes.
problems.inputFormat: A single integer N.

problems.title: Remove Duplicates from List
problems.description: Given a list of integers, remove duplicates while maintaining the original order of first occurrences.
problems.inputFormat:
First line: Integer N.
Second line: N space-separated integers.
problems.outputFormat: Print the list without duplicates, space-separated.

problems.title: Prime Checker Using Function
problems.description:
Given a number N, check if it is prime using a function.

A prime number is greater than 1 and has no divisors other than 1 and itself.
problems.inputFormat: A single positive integer N.
problems.outputFormat: Print 'PRIME' if N is prime, otherwise 'NOT PRIME'.
problems.explanation: 17 is only divisible by 1 and 17. Hence prime.

problems.title: Count Words in String
problems.description: Given a string, count the number of words. Words are separated by one or more spaces.
problems.inputFormat: A single line string.

problems.title: Sum of Digits Until Single
problems.description:
Given a non-negative integer N, repeatedly sum its digits until a single digit is obtained.

For example: 9875 → 9+8+7+5 = 29 → 2+9 = 11 → 1+1 = 2
problems.inputFormat: A single non-negative integer N.

problems.title: Dictionary Key-Value Swap
problems.description:
Given a dictionary with unique values, swap keys and values.

Input will be given as key:value pairs.
problems.inputFormat:
First line: Integer N (number of pairs).
Next N lines: Each line contains 'key value' separated by space.
problems.outputFormat: Print swapped pairs in alphabetical order of new keys (original values), format: 'new_key new_value'.
problems.explanation: Original: {a:1, b:2, c:3}. Swapped: {1:a, 2:b, 3:c}. Sorted by keys.

problems.title: Matrix Transpose
problems.description:
Given a matrix of size N x M, compute and print its transpose.

The transpose of a matrix is obtained by swapping rows with columns.
problems.inputFormat:
First line: Two integers N and M.
Next N lines: M space-separated integers each.
problems.outputFormat: Print the transposed matrix (M lines, each with N integers).

problems.title: Two Sum Problem
problems.description:
Given a list of integers and a target sum, find two distinct indices such that their elements add up to the target.

Return the indices (0-indexed). If multiple solutions exist, return any one. If no solution exists, print -1.
problems.inputFormat:
First line: Two integers N and target.
Second line: N space-separated integers.

problems.title: Group Anagrams
problems.description:
Given a list of strings, group the anagrams together.

Anagrams are strings that contain the same characters with the same frequencies.
problems.inputFormat:
First line: Integer N.
Second line: N space-separated strings (lowercase letters).
problems.outputFormat: Print each group on a new line, with strings space-separated. Groups should be in order of first occurrence.
problems.sampleInput:
6
eat tea tan ate nat bat
problems.sampleOutput:
eat tea ate
tan nat
bat
problems.explanation: eat, tea, ate are anagrams. tan, nat are anagrams. bat is alone.

testCases.input:
6
eat tea tan ate nat bat
testCases.expectedOutput:
eat tea ate
tan nat
bat

problems.title: Flatten Nested List
problems.description:
Given a nested list structure, flatten it to a single-level list.

For simplicity, input is given as a string representation of nested list.
problems.inputFormat: A single line representing nested list (e.g., '[1,[2,[3,4]],5]').
problems.outputFormat: Print the flattened list elements, space-separated.

problems.title: Longest Common Prefix
problems.description:
Given a list of strings, find the longest common prefix among all strings.

If there is no common prefix, return an empty string.
problems.inputFormat:
First line: Integer N.
Second line: N space-separated strings.
problems.outputFormat: Print the longest common prefix or 'NONE' if empty.
problems.explanation: Common prefix in flower, flow, flight is 'fl'.

problems.title: Valid Parentheses
problems.description:
Given a string containing only '(', ')', '{', '}', '[', ']', determine if the input string is valid.

A string is valid if:
1. Open brackets are closed by the same type.
2. Open brackets are closed in the correct order.
problems.inputFormat: A single string of brackets.
problems.explanation: All brackets are properly matched and ordered.

problems.title: Merge Intervals
problems.description:
Given a collection of intervals, merge all overlapping intervals.

Intervals are given as pairs [start, end].
problems.inputFormat:
First line: Integer N.
Next N lines: Two integers start and end for each interval.
problems.outputFormat: Print merged intervals, each on a new line as 'start end'.
problems.explanation: [1,3] and [2,6] overlap, merged to [1,6]. Others don't overlap.

problems.title: Generate Permutations
problems.description:
Given a list of N distinct integers, generate all possible permutations.

Print each permutation on a new line in lexicographical order.
problems.inputFormat:
First line: Integer N.
Second line: N space-separated distinct integers.
problems.outputFormat: Print all permutations, one per line, in lexicographical order.
problems.explanation: All 6 permutations of [1,2,3] in lexicographical order.

problems.title: Longest Substring Without Repeat
problems.description: Given a string, find the length of the longest substring without repeating characters.
problems.inputFormat: A single string.
problems.outputFormat: Print the length of the longest substring without repeating characters.
problems.explanation: The longest substring without repeating is 'abc' with length 3.

problems.title: Spiral Matrix Order
problems.description: Given an N x M matrix, return all elements in spiral order (clockwise starting from top-left).
problems.inputFormat:
First line: Two integers N and M.
Next N lines: M space-separated integers.
problems.outputFormat: Print all elements in spiral order, space-separated.
problems.explanation: Spiral: top row (1,2,3) → right column (6,9) → bottom row reverse (8,7) → left column up (4) → center (5).

problems.title: Generate Subsets (Power Set)
problems.description:
Given a list of N distinct integers, generate all possible subsets (the power set).

Print each subset on a new line in lexicographical order. Empty subset is represented as 'EMPTY'.
problems.inputFormat:
First line: Integer N.
Second line: N space-separated distinct integers.
problems.outputFormat: Print all subsets, one per line, space-separated elements. Print 'EMPTY' for empty subset.
problems.explanation: All 2^3 = 8 subsets of [1,2,3] in lexicographical order.

problems.title: Word Ladder Length
problems.description:
Given two words (beginWord and endWord) and a dictionary of words, find the length of the shortest transformation sequence from beginWord to endWord.

Rules:
1. Only one letter can be changed at a time.
2. Each transformed word must exist in the dictionary.

Return 0 if no such transformation exists.
problems.inputFormat:
First line: beginWord and endWord separated by space.
Second line: Integer N (dictionary size).
Third line: N dictionary words separated by space.
problems.outputFormat: Print the length of shortest transformation sequence.
problems.sampleInput:
hit cog
6
hot dot dog lot log cog
problems.explanation: hit → hot → dot → dog → cog. Length = 5.

testCases.input:
hit cog
6
hot dot dog lot log cog

hit cog
5
hot dot dog lot log

cat dog
8
bat bag bog bog dog cot cog cat

problems.title: Knapsack 0/1 Problem
problems.description:
Given weights and values of N items, put these items in a knapsack of capacity W to get the maximum total value.

Each item can only be selected once (0/1 property).
problems.inputFormat:
First line: Two integers N and W.
Second line: N space-separated weights.
Third line: N space-separated values.
problems.outputFormat: Print the maximum value that can be obtained.
problems.explanation: Take items with weight 20 (value 100) and weight 30 (value 120). Total weight = 50, value = 220.

problems.title: Basic SELECT Query
problems.description:
Write a SQL query to select the 'name' and 'age' columns from a table called 'students' where age is greater than 18.

Table: students (id, name, age, grade)
problems.inputFormat: Table 'students' with columns: id, name, age, grade
problems.outputFormat: name, age columns for students older than 18
problems.explanation: SELECT name, age FROM students WHERE age > 18

testCases.input: SELECT name, age FROM students WHERE age > 18
SELECT NAME, AGE FROM STUDENTS WHERE AGE > 18
select name, age from students where age > 18;
SELECT name, age FROM students WHERE age >= 19

problems.title: ORDER BY Clause
problems.description: Write a SQL query to select all columns from 'employees' table and order the results by 'salary' in descending order.
problems.inputFormat: Table 'employees' with columns: id, name, department, salary
problems.outputFormat: All columns sorted by salary (highest first)
problems.explanation: SELECT * FROM employees ORDER BY salary DESC

testCases.input: SELECT * FROM employees ORDER BY salary DESC
SELECT * FROM employees ORDER BY salary DESC;
select * from employees order by salary desc

problems.title: COUNT Aggregate Function
problems.description: Write a SQL query to count the total number of rows in the 'orders' table.
problems.inputFormat: Table 'orders' with columns: order_id, customer_id, amount, order_date
problems.outputFormat: Single value: total count of orders

problems.title: DISTINCT Values
problems.description: Write a SQL query to select all unique/distinct values from the 'category' column in the 'products' table.
problems.inputFormat: Table 'products' with columns: product_id, name, category, price

problems.title: LIKE Pattern Matching
problems.description: Write a SQL query to find all customers whose name starts with 'A' from the 'customers' table.
problems.inputFormat: Table 'customers' with columns: customer_id, name, email, city
problems.outputFormat: All columns for customers with names starting with 'A'
problems.explanation: SELECT * FROM customers WHERE name LIKE 'A%'

testCases.input: SELECT * FROM customers WHERE name LIKE 'A%'
SELECT * FROM customers WHERE name LIKE 'a%'
select * from customers where name like 'A%';

problems.title: BETWEEN Range Query
problems.description: Write a SQL query to find all products with price between 100 and 500 (inclusive) from the 'products' table.
problems.inputFormat: Table 'products' with columns: product_id, name, category, price
problems.explanation: SELECT * FROM products WHERE price BETWEEN 100 AND 500

testCases.input: SELECT * FROM products WHERE price BETWEEN 100 AND 500
SELECT * FROM products WHERE price >= 100 AND price <= 500
select * from products where price between 100 and 500;

problems.title: SUM Aggregate Function
problems.description: Write a SQL query to calculate the total sum of 'amount' column from the 'orders' table.
problems.inputFormat: Table 'orders' with columns: order_id, customer_id, amount, order_date
problems.explanation: SELECT SUM(amount) AS total_amount FROM orders

testCases.input: SELECT SUM(amount) AS total FROM orders

problems.title: IN Operator
problems.description: Write a SQL query to find all employees who work in 'Sales', 'Marketing', or 'IT' departments.
problems.inputFormat: Table 'employees' with columns: id, name, department, salary
problems.sampleOutput: Employees in Sales, Marketing, or IT
problems.explanation: SELECT * FROM employees WHERE department IN ('Sales', 'Marketing', 'IT')

testCases.input: SELECT * FROM employees WHERE department IN ('Sales', 'Marketing', 'IT')
SELECT * FROM employees WHERE department = 'Sales' OR department = 'Marketing' OR department = 'IT'
select * from employees where department in ('Sales', 'Marketing', 'IT');

problems.title: GROUP BY with COUNT
problems.description: Write a SQL query to count the number of employees in each department from the 'employees' table.
problems.inputFormat: Table 'employees' with columns: id, name, department, salary
problems.outputFormat: department name and count of employees
problems.explanation: SELECT department, COUNT(*) FROM employees GROUP BY department

testCases.input: SELECT department, COUNT(*) FROM employees GROUP BY department
SELECT department, COUNT(id) AS count FROM employees GROUP BY department
select department, count(*) from employees group by department;

problems.title: INNER JOIN Two Tables
problems.description: Write a SQL query to join 'orders' and 'customers' tables to get customer name with their order amount.
problems.inputFormat:
Table 'orders' (order_id, customer_id, amount)
Table 'customers' (customer_id, name, email)
problems.sampleInput: orders and customers tables with matching customer_id
problems.explanation: SELECT c.name, o.amount FROM orders o INNER JOIN customers c ON o.customer_id = c.customer_id

testCases.input: SELECT c.name, o.amount FROM orders o INNER JOIN customers c ON o.customer_id = c.customer_id
SELECT customers.name, orders.amount FROM orders JOIN customers ON orders.customer_id = customers.customer_id
select c.name, o.amount from orders o inner join customers c on o.customer_id = c.customer_id;

problems.title: HAVING Clause
problems.description: Write a SQL query to find departments with more than 5 employees from the 'employees' table.
problems.inputFormat: Table 'employees' with columns: id, name, department, salary
problems.explanation: SELECT department, COUNT(*) FROM employees GROUP BY department HAVING COUNT(*) > 5

testCases.input: SELECT department, COUNT(*) FROM employees GROUP BY department HAVING COUNT(*) > 5
SELECT department, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM employees GROUP BY department HAVING cnt > 5
select department, count(*) from employees group by department having count(*) > 5;

problems.title: LEFT JOIN
problems.description: Write a SQL query to get all customers and their orders (if any). Include customers who haven't placed any orders.
problems.inputFormat:
Table 'customers' (customer_id, name)
Table 'orders' (order_id, customer_id, amount)
problems.outputFormat: customer name with order amount (NULL if no orders)
problems.explanation: SELECT c.name, o.amount FROM customers c LEFT JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id

testCases.input: SELECT c.name, o.amount FROM customers c LEFT JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id
SELECT customers.name, orders.amount FROM customers LEFT OUTER JOIN orders ON customers.customer_id = orders.customer_id
select c.name, o.amount from customers c left join orders o on c.customer_id = o.customer_id;

problems.title: Subquery in WHERE
problems.description: Write a SQL query to find employees who earn more than the average salary.
problems.inputFormat: Table 'employees' with columns: id, name, department, salary
problems.explanation: SELECT * FROM employees WHERE salary > (SELECT AVG(salary) FROM employees)

testCases.input: SELECT * FROM employees WHERE salary > (SELECT AVG(salary) FROM employees)
SELECT name, salary FROM employees WHERE salary > (SELECT AVG(salary) FROM employees)
select * from employees where salary > (select avg(salary) from employees);

problems.title: CASE Statement
problems.description: Write a SQL query to categorize employees by salary: 'High' for salary > 50000, 'Medium' for salary > 30000, else 'Low'.
problems.inputFormat: Table 'employees' with columns: id, name, salary
problems.sampleOutput:
name  | category
------|--------
John  | High
Jane  | Medium
problems.explanation: SELECT name, CASE WHEN salary > 50000 THEN 'High' WHEN salary > 30000 THEN 'Medium' ELSE 'Low' END AS category FROM employees

testCases.input: SELECT name, CASE WHEN salary > 50000 THEN 'High' WHEN salary > 30000 THEN 'Medium' ELSE 'Low' END AS category FROM employees
SELECT name, CASE WHEN salary > 50000 THEN 'High' WHEN salary > 30000 THEN 'Medium' ELSE 'Low' END FROM employees

problems.title: UPDATE Statement
problems.description: Write a SQL query to increase the salary by 10% for all employees in the 'IT' department.
problems.inputFormat: Table 'employees' with columns: id, name, department, salary
problems.outputFormat: UPDATE statement affecting IT department employees
problems.explanation: UPDATE employees SET salary = salary * 1.10 WHERE department = 'IT'

testCases.input: UPDATE employees SET salary = salary * 1.10 WHERE department = 'IT'
UPDATE employees SET salary = salary * 1.1 WHERE department = 'IT'
update employees set salary = salary * 1.10 where department = 'IT';

problems.title: DELETE with Condition
problems.description: Write a SQL query to delete all orders that were placed before '2023-01-01'.
problems.inputFormat: Table 'orders' with columns: order_id, order_date, amount
problems.constraints: Use DELETE with WHERE and date comparison

problems.title: Multiple Table JOIN
problems.description: Write a SQL query to get product name, category name, and order amount by joining three tables: products, categories, and order_items.
problems.inputFormat:
Table 'products' (product_id, name, category_id)
Table 'categories' (category_id, category_name)
Table 'order_items' (item_id, order_id, product_id, quantity, price)
problems.explanation: SELECT p.name, c.category_name, oi.quantity * oi.price AS amount FROM order_items oi JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.product_id JOIN categories c ON p.category_id = c.category_id

testCases.input: SELECT p.name, c.category_name, oi.quantity * oi.price AS amount FROM order_items oi JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.product_id JOIN categories c ON p.category_id = c.category_id

SELECT products.name, categories.category_name, order_items.quantity * order_items.price FROM order_items INNER JOIN products ON order_items.product_id = products.product_id INNER JOIN categories ON products.category_id = categories.category_id

problems.title: Self JOIN
problems.description: Write a SQL query to find employees who have the same manager. Display pairs of employee names and their manager's name.
problems.inputFormat:
Table 'employees' with columns: id, name, manager_id
Note: manager_id refers to id in the same table
problems.sampleOutput: John, Jane, Bob (both report to Bob)
problems.explanation: SELECT e1.name, e2.name, m.name AS manager FROM employees e1 JOIN employees e2 ON e1.manager_id = e2.manager_id AND e1.id < e2.id JOIN employees m ON e1.manager_id = m.id

testCases.input: SELECT e1.name, e2.name, m.name AS manager FROM employees e1 JOIN employees e2 ON e1.manager_id = e2.manager_id AND e1.id < e2.id JOIN employees m ON e1.manager_id = m.id
SELECT e1.name AS emp1, e2.name AS emp2, m.name AS mgr FROM employees e1, employees e2, employees m WHERE e1.manager_id = e2.manager_id AND e1.id < e2.id AND e1.manager_id = m.id

problems.title: Window Function - RANK
problems.description: Write a SQL query to rank employees by salary within each department. Use RANK() window function.
problems.inputFormat: Table 'employees' with columns: id, name, department, salary
problems.constraints: Use RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY ... ORDER BY ...)
problems.explanation: SELECT name, department, salary, RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY department ORDER BY salary DESC) AS rank FROM employees

testCases.input: SELECT name, department, salary, RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY department ORDER BY salary DESC) AS rank FROM employees
SELECT name, department, salary, RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY department ORDER BY salary DESC) FROM employees

problems.title: CTE (Common Table Expression)
problems.description: Write a SQL query using CTE to find the top 3 highest-paid employees in each department.
problems.inputFormat: Table 'employees' with columns: id, name, department, salary
problems.outputFormat: Top 3 employees per department by salary
problems.explanation: WITH ranked AS (SELECT *, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY department ORDER BY salary DESC) as rn FROM employees) SELECT * FROM ranked WHERE rn <= 3

testCases.input: WITH ranked AS (SELECT *, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY department ORDER BY salary DESC) as rn FROM employees) SELECT * FROM ranked WHERE rn <= 3
WITH ranked AS (SELECT name, department, salary, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY department ORDER BY salary DESC) as rank FROM employees) SELECT name, department, salary FROM ranked WHERE rank <= 3
